_comment: Allowlist for scripts/color-literal-gate.mjs (npm run color:gate). Scope: src/**/*.ts excluding src/cli/theme/**. Counts = occurrences of hex color literals (#rgb/#rrggbb/#rrggbbaa) and SGR color escapes (\x1b[3Xm, \x1b[9Xm, 38;2;, 38;5;, 48;2;, 48;5;). Keep file keys sorted so CI diffs stay deterministic.

permanent._comment: Exempt by design. (1) src/commands/init* template payload files: these hex values are generated-project content (scaffolds written into user repos), not OMK render debt. (2) src/{adapters/,}kimi/ascii-art.ts: pure data modules holding a pre-rendered truecolor pixel-art banner (half-block cells, one fg+bg SGR pair per cell, 2016 escapes each). The escapes ARE the image bytes — regenerating the color ramp from theme tokens would corrupt the artwork, so the raw bytes are kept verbatim per theme contract T5b option (b). (3) src/theme/extended-palette.ts: the single data-only color module declaring VALUES with no night-city primitive (bespoke sigil/sweep neon ramps, the ontology graph-viewer web/SVG palette, and the DESIGN.md scaffold defaults). Every other source file imports these tokens instead of inlining hex, so the literals live in exactly one reviewed place; it imports BRAND_HEX as the theme-derived base and contains no render logic. Neither ascii-art module is imported at runtime today, so they carry zero terminal-output risk.

legacy-burn-down._comment: TEMPORARY ratchet ceilings (theme contract phase 0). The gate fails if a file's hex or sgr count rises above its ceiling, or if a new violating file appears. Counts may only DECREASE — lower the ceiling (or delete the entry) as migration moves files onto theme tokens. EMPTIED 2026-06-10 by Lane G1-BURNDOWN: all 10 prior entries migrated to theme-derived lookups (BRAND_HEX / numeric SGR codes) or the src/theme/extended-palette.ts data module. Section kept for future sanctioned burn-down work only; do not add new entries except for that.
